_meta.purpose: Curated source registry for the CityBriefAgent: per-CoC lead agency, current strategic/care plan, and situation context, with real citations. Grounds general-knowledge answers about each city's homelessness situation and response strategy. Population-level / system-level only — never individual data.
_meta.verification_note: Every URL below was surfaced by a live web search on 2026-06-16. Items flagged "verify": true should be re-confirmed before the demo (the named plan exists, but the canonical link may have moved). Counts come from this repo's data/coc_panel.csv (HUD 2024 PIT) unless noted.
_meta.usage_rule: The agent must cite the source(s) it used and must not state a figure or claim not traceable to a listed source or the project's own data files.

national_frameworks.title: All In: The Federal Strategic Plan to Prevent and End Homelessness (USICH)
national_frameworks.note: Federal blueprint: 6 pillars, ~30 strategies, ~180 actions; Housing First; short-term goal to reduce homelessness 25% by 2025; explicit equity / systemic-racism focus. The framing most local plans align to.

national_frameworks.title: HUD Continuum of Care (CoC) Program
national_frameworks.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
national_frameworks.note: The federal program that funds and structures every local CoC referenced below; defines the annual PIT count, CoC governance, and McKinney-Vento funding.

cities.plan_title: LAHSA Strategic Vision / LA CoC (incl. LA County Homeless Initiative)

key_sources.title: LAHSA — Los Angeles Homeless Services Authority

cities.situation_note: Largest CoC in the panel; ~71,201 people homeless in the 2024 PIT, majority unsheltered. Strategy emphasizes a redesigned Coordinated Entry System, Time-Limited Subsidies, and unsheltered-focused funding (incl. a $60M HUD award).

cities.plan_title: Sacramento Local Homeless Action Plan (LHAP) + 'All In' Regionally Coordinated Homeless Action Plan (RCHAP)

key_sources.title: Plan to Address Homelessness in Sacramento — Sacramento Steps Forward
All In: Regional Coordinated Homeless Action Plan (PDF)

cities.situation_note: Three-year LHAP (2022–2025) plus a 2024 regionally coordinated plan (RCHAP, 'All In') across city, county, CoC, and SHRA; community-wide response system standards published Dec 2023.

cities.lead_agency: Tucson Pima Collaboration to End Homelessness (TPCH)
cities.plan_title: TPCH Strategic Plan 2026–2030 + 'Bringing Pima Home' Community Plan

key_sources.title: Bringing Pima Home — 2023 Update to the Community Plan (PDF)
City of Tucson — TPCH 5-year strategic plan

cities.situation_note: TPCH coordinates the CoC (does not deliver direct services). A new 2026–2030 strategic plan was adopted; the 'Bringing Pima Home' community plan organizes prevention, housing, and supportive-services strategy.

cities.coc_name: San Diego City and County CoC
cities.plan_title: Regional Community Action Plan to Prevent and End Homelessness ('Plan of Plans')

key_sources.title: Community Action Plan on Homelessness for the City of San Diego — SDHC

cities.situation_note: RTFH's regional action plan is a five-year 'Plan of Plans' integrating federal/state/local plans; goals include ending veteran/youth/family/senior homelessness and halving unsheltered homelessness; identifies a need for ~9,000+ units and added shelter beds.

cities.lead_agency: SF Department of Homelessness and Supportive Housing (HSH)
cities.plan_title: Home by the Bay: Citywide Strategic Plan 2023–2028

key_sources.title: Home by the Bay 2023–2028 (full plan PDF)
Home by the Bay: Year One Progress Report (Oct 2024)

cities.situation_note: Equity-driven 5-year plan with five goals (e.g. 30,000 moves to permanent housing; 85% non-return; prevention for 18,000). Year-one: 5,256 people moved to housing; 83% did not return within 24 months.

cities.plan_title: KCRHA Five-Year Plan (2023–2028, addendum extending to 2029)
cities.situation_note: Six goals incl. expanding temporary/permanent housing, restructuring the service system, reducing the impact of racism, and preventing family/youth homelessness; a 2024 addendum aligns to WA Dept of Commerce requirements and extends to 2029.

cities.lead_agency: Maricopa Association of Governments (MAG) — Maricopa Regional CoC
cities.plan_title: Regional Plan to End Homelessness + 'Pathways Home' regional action plan
cities.situation_note: MAG has staffed the CoC since 1999; >$400M in funding across 30+ programs. Vision: homelessness rare, brief, non-recurring. 'Pathways Home' is the regional action plan to reduce homelessness across Maricopa County.

cities.coc_name: San Jose/Santa Clara City & County CoC
cities.lead_agency: Santa Clara County Office of Supportive Housing (with Destination: Home)
cities.plan_title: Community Plan to End Homelessness (2020–2025; 2025–2030 in development)

key_sources.title: Community Plan to End Homelessness — Office of Supportive Housing
Homelessness Response — City of San José
Our Community's Supportive Housing System — Destination: Home

cities.situation_note: Three core strategies: address root causes via system/policy change, expand prevention + housing, and improve quality of life for unsheltered residents. ~27,000 obtained temporary housing and ~38,000 received prevention services under the plan.

cities.lead_agency: Joint Office of Homeless Services (City of Portland + Multnomah County)
cities.plan_title: Homelessness Response Action Plan (HRAP, 2024) + Multnomah Local Implementation Plan

key_sources.title: Multnomah County + Portland plan to halve unsheltered homelessness by 2026 — OPB (Mar 2024)
Joint Office of Homeless Services audit — Multnomah County

cities.situation_note: JOHS (est. 2016) centralizes planning and funding. 2024 HRAP goal: halve unsheltered homelessness by 2026, add shelter beds and affordable housing, expand behavioral-health treatment, and improve data and oversight.

cities.coc_name: Las Vegas/Clark County CoC (Southern Nevada Homelessness CoC)
cities.lead_agency: Southern Nevada Homelessness CoC — 'Help Hope Home' (Clark County administers)
cities.plan_title: HELP HOPE HOME: Southern Nevada's Regional Plan to End Homelessness

key_sources.title: Southern Nevada Homelessness CoC — Help Hope Home

cities.situation_note: HUD CoC NV-500 covering Clark County; SNHCoC Board develops the 'Help Hope Home' regional plan. ~$25M CoC funding (~$19.5M to permanent supportive housing). 2024 PIT: 7,906 people — +20% over 2023, a decade high. $200M 'Campus for Hope' shelter planned (ops from 2027).

cities.lead_agency: NYC Department of Social Services / Department of Homeless Services (DSS-DHS)
cities.plan_title: Housing Our Neighbors: A Blueprint for Housing and Homelessness (2022)
cities.situation_note: Largest sheltered system in the country (right-to-shelter); very low unsheltered rate (HOPE 2024 estimated ~4,140 unsheltered). 2022 'Housing Our Neighbors' blueprint sets housing + homelessness strategy; CityFHEPS rental subsidy is a core tool.

cities.lead_agency: DC Interagency Council on Homelessness (ICH) + The Community Partnership (TCP)
cities.plan_title: Homeward DC 2.0: ICH Strategic Plan FY2021–FY2025
cities.situation_note: Vision: homelessness rare, brief, non-recurring; 100+ strategies under twelve goals; explicit aim to eliminate racial inequities. Built on HMIS data. Led collaboratively by ICH, TCP, and people with lived experience.

cities.lead_agency: Hennepin County (Office to End Homelessness) — 'Heading Home Hennepin'
cities.plan_title: Heading Home Hennepin — CoC plan to prevent and end homelessness

key_sources.title: Continuum of Care partnership to end homelessness — Hennepin County

cities.situation_note: Hennepin County is the HUD collaborative applicant (~$16M/yr to 40+ projects). The Office to End Homelessness (jointly supported by Minneapolis + Hennepin County) coordinates the CoC; strategy emphasizes prevention and protective factors for at-risk residents.

cities.lead_agency: Knoxville-Knox County Office of Housing Stability (OHS) + CoC Board
cities.plan_title: Knoxville-Knox County Three-Year Plan to Address Homelessness (2025)

key_sources.title: Knoxville-Knox County CoC Info — City of Knoxville
Office of Housing Stability unveils 3-year plan (WVLT, Sep 2025)

cities.situation_note: Smallest CoC in the panel; OHS launched in 2023 after homelessness rose ~50% the prior year. A new CoC Board adopted a measurable Three-Year Plan (2025) with HUD-aligned performance metrics; goal: homelessness rare, brief, non-recurring.

cities.plan_title: Priority Home: Miami-Dade County Community Homeless Plan
cities.situation_note: Funded by the nation's first dedicated homelessness tax (1992 Food & Beverage tax; 85% to ending homelessness). 'Priority Home' aims for rare/brief/one-time homelessness; HUD approved a coordinated youth-homelessness plan (Sep 2024); new prevention partnership ('Right at Home').

cities.lead_agency: City of Philadelphia Office of Homeless Services (OHS)
cities.plan_title: Roadmap to Homes: Philadelphia's Five-Year Strategic Plan
cities.situation_note: 'Roadmap to Homes' targets a 5%/year reduction in street homelessness; three overarching goals (rare, brief, non-recurring) and five primary goals incl. expanding housing resources, integrating systems, and engaging landlords.

cities.lead_agency: Chicago Dept. of Family & Support Services (DFSS) + All Chicago (CoC)
cities.plan_title: Plan 2.0: A Home for Everyone + draft Five-Year Blueprint on Homelessness 2026–2031

key_sources.title: Chicago Continuum of Care — City of Chicago
Chicago's Plan to End Homelessness — Plan 2.0: A Home for Everyone
DRAFT — Chicago Five-Year Blueprint on Homelessness 2026–2031 (PDF)

cities.situation_note: DFSS manages prevention/shelter/outreach/housing with the Chicago CoC (All Chicago). 'Plan 2.0' (Housing First, prevention, wraparound) is being succeeded by a new Five-Year Blueprint (2026–2031, draft).
